data.speaker: narrator
data.text: The Keeper sets the pot on the empty Home Plot. A short while later Greg ambles up the path, Blue padding behind.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Ah, you found a good spot for it. Sun on it most of the day, shelter at its back. The seed will like that.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Now then. A seed is a small, sleepy thing. It does not need much. It needs the same things you do. A drink. A little warmth. Somebody to notice it.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Water first. Not a flood, just enough to wet the soil. A seed that drowns will not thank you, and a seed left dry will simply sleep.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Then the mana. Hold your hands close and let a little of the Ather pass through. You will feel it go. Like warming your palms at a fire, only the fire is on your side of it.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: That is all there is to it. Water, warmth, a little of your time. Do it each day and do not rush.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: It will not bloom today. Maybe not tomorrow. Good things take the time they take. Come find me when you are restless, and I will give you something to do while it grows.
